instruction: Download Stats NZ monthly CSV. Open VTW_Trade_Monthly tab. Paste rows below the last data row. Do NOT edit row 1 (headers are sacred).
example: Paste 12 new rows for 2024-01 through 2024-12. Set series = nz_export.

instruction: Open the _keywords tab. Add a new row with: term (the keyword), type (allow or block), language (ko or en). Save. Keywords take effect on the next scheduled build.
Open the GitHub repository Actions tab at github.com/wkdjk/velvet-knowledge-hub/actions. A red X means the last build failed. You will also receive an alert email if ALERT_EMAIL is configured.
example: Look for a red circle icon on the most recent workflow run.

instruction: Never edit, move, or delete row 1 in any data tab. Row 1 contains the column headers that all scripts depend on. Editing row 1 will break the pipeline.
example: If a header looks wrong, contact TechQ - do not edit it yourself.

instruction: Create a new Sheets tab with the correct headers (row 1). Then add one YAML block to config.yaml in the repo. No Python edits required. See config.yaml for the block format.
example: id: my_new_source, tab: My_New_Tab, kind: records, section: import_intelligence, enabled: true

instruction: Download the MFDS 수입식품 정보마루 CSV for the latest quarter. Open VFI_Import_Records tab. Paste rows below the last existing row. The build script deduplicates on (date, importer, product_name).
example: Paste Q3 2024 rows. Duplicates from earlier quarters are automatically skipped on the next build.

instruction: The Source_Status tab tracks when each source was last updated. After pasting new data into any source tab, update the last_updated cell in the corresponding Source_Status row to today's date (YYYY-MM-DD).
example: Find the nz_export row in Source_Status. Update last_updated to 2024-09-30.

instruction: MFDS publishes annual deer velvet price rankings once per year. Download the annual report PDF, extract the ranking table, and paste into VFI_Price_Annual. Dedup key is (year, rank, product_name).
example: Paste 2024 annual rankings. Year column = 2024. Rank = 1, 2, 3 in order.
